snippet.title: Occidental CEO Hollub envisions the opportunity to license 1,000 carbon capture plants #OXYStock
snippet.description:
Vicki Hollub, CEO of Occidental, mentioned that the company's direct air capture business could potentially facilitate over 1,000 projects to address global warming. 

This could lead to a new revenue stream by selling DAC technology and design expertise.

"We will be licensing a lot of this out," Hollub reportedly said on the company's post-earnings conference call.

The CEO said more than 65% of the first plant's capacity through 2030 has been sold to partners, otherwise there is "absolutely no way that we would have the capability to provide all the capital," as reported by Reuters.

Under its franchise-style approach, Occidental plans to levy a licensing fee and delegate management and construction responsibilities to partners. 

The company's initial $1.3 billion DAC project on a large scale is anticipated to commence in mid-2025.

☆Who is Charlie Munger?☆
Charlie Munger is vice chairman of Berkshire Hathaway and the closest partner of Warren Buffett. He is one of the best investors ever existed with his profound wisdom in investing, business and life. Prior joining Warren Buffett at Berkshire Hathaway, Charlie Munger ran an investment partnership that returned an average of 20% per year from 1962 to 1975. Meanwhile, the S&P 500 averaged an annual return of just 5% in that time.

snippet.title: Warren Buffett Reveals Why He Invested $10 Billion in Occidental Petroleum
snippet.description:
n this video, billionaire investor Warren Buffett shares his insights on why he decided to lend $10 billion to Occidental Petroleum in 2019 to help finance its takeover of Anadarko Petroleum.

snippet.title: Success Lessons from Warren Buffett and Bill Gates
snippet.description:
Listen to The personally revealing and complete biography of the man known everywhere as The Oracle of Omaha. Here is the Audio book recounting the life and times of one of the most respected men in the world, Warren Buffett.

The Snowball : Warren Buffett and the Business of Life By Alice Schroeder

Get the book here :      https://amzn.to/3J3EA0w


Bill Gates and Warren Buffett have been best friends for 27 years.They are the second and third-wealthiest people on the planet. They also happen to be best friends. But when they met 27 years ago, it was grudgingly. Each one was surprised at how much they enjoyed talking to the other.

Initially, they weren't interested in meeting each other - but they hit it off right away.They've partnered on political and philanthropic ventures, mentored each other, and had lots of laughs.

"Success Lessons from Warren Buffett and Bill Gates"

snippet.title: Columbia Global Energy Summit 2023
snippet.description:
To kick off the celebration of our 10th anniversary, CGEP hosted a special 10th Anniversary Global Energy Summit — where we convened some of the brightest minds and important leaders to discuss turbulence in global energy markets, the growing gap between climate ambition and reality, and the tensions between today’s energy needs and tomorrow’s energy transition imperative.

0:37:59 - Welcome Remarks | Keren Yarhi-Milo (Dean, School of International and Public Affairs (SIPA), Adlai E. Stevenson Professor of International Relations, Columbia University)

0:45:00 - Opening Remarks | Jason Bordoff (Founding Director, Center on Global Energy Policy; Professor of Professional Practice, Columbia SIPA; Co-Founding Dean, Columbia Climate School)

0:53:50 - Panel | Power Play: The State of Energy Geopolitics Amidst Tensions and Transitions
- Ian Bremmer (President, Eurasia Group and GZERO Media)
- Meghan L. O'Sullivan (Director-designate, Belfer Center for Science and International Affairs and Jeane Kirkpatrick Professor of the Practice of International Affairs)
- Ana Palacio (Former Foreign Minister of Spain)
- Moderated by Daniel Yergin (Vice Chairman, IHS Markit)

1:37:55 - Building America's New Energy Future: A Conversation with Jennifer M. Granholm, Secretary, U.S. Department of Energy
- Moderated by Jason Bordoff

2:09:35 - Panel | Mobilizing Global Climate Action: Accelerating The Path to Net Zero by 2050
- Manish Bapna (CEO & President, NRDC)
- Representative John Curtis (US Congress)
- Fred Krupp (President, Environmental Defense Fund)
- Laurence Tubiana (CEO, European Climate Foundation)
- Moderated by Lisa Friedman (New York Times)

3:06:11 - Balancing Growth, Sustainability, and Access to Power Africa: A Conversation with H.E. Paul Kagame, President of the Republic of Rwanda
- Moderated by Jason Bordoff

3:47:05 - Panel | Building a Sustainable Future in Developing Countries
- Andrew Kamau (Managing Director of International Programs, Energy Opportunity Lab, Center on Global Energy Policy)
- Damilola Ogunbiyi (CEO of Sustainable Energy for All , Special Representative, UN Secretary-General for Sustainable Energy for All, and Co-Chair, UN-Energy)
- Hon. Okaasai Sidronius Opolot (Minister of State for Energy, Republic of Uganda)
- Moderated by Jonathan Elkind (Senior Research Scholar, Center on Global Energy Policy)

4:23:23 - Panel | Innovating to Net Zero: The Role of Technology in the Energy Transition
- Emmanuel Lagarrigue (Partner, KKR Infrastructure)
- Dr. Arun Majumdar (Chester Naramore Dean of the Stanford Doerr School of Sustainability, Jay Precourt Professor of Mechanical Engineering and of Energy Science & Engineering, and Senior Fellow of the Precourt Institute for Energy and of the Hoover Institution)
- Ann Mettler (Vice-President, Europe, Breakthrough Energy)
- Nobuo Tanaka (Chair, Steering Committee, Innovation for Cool Earth Forum, Executive Director Emeritus, the International Energy Agency (IEA) and former Distinguished Fellow, Center on Global Energy Policy, Columbia University)
- Moderated by Vijay Vaitheeswaran (Global Energy & Climate Innovation Editor, The Economist)

5:49:33 - Financing the Global Energy Transition: A Conversation with Larry Fink, Chairman and Chief Executive Officer, BlackRock, Inc.
- Moderated by Jason Bordoff

6:25:35 - Navigating the Disorderly Energy Transition: A Conversation with Fatih Birol, Executive Director, IEA
- Moderated by Jason Bordoff

6:58:24 - The Role of Today's Largest Companies in Tomorrow’s Energy Landscape: A Conversation with Patrick Pouyanné, Chairman of the Board and Chief Executive Officer, TotalEnergies
- Moderated by Jason Bordoff

7:39:13 - Panel | The Big Green Machine: Advancing the Energy Transition at Scale
- Mads Nipper (Group President and CEO, Ørsted)
- Christian Bruch (CEO, Siemens Energy)
- Moderated by Amy Harder (Executive Editor, Cipher News)

8:08:37 - Panel | Meeting the Energy and Climate Needs of Emerging Economies
- HE Leila Benali (Minister of Energy Transition and Sustainable Development, Government of Morocco)
- MA Jun (Founding Director, Institute of Public and Environmental Affairs)
- Sumant Sinha (Chairman & CEO, ReNew Power)
- Moderated by Akshat Rathi (Bloomberg)

8:45:16 - Panel | The Global State of Play: Energy Markets, Geopolitics, Policy, and What's Next
- Vicki Hollub (President and Chief Executive Officer, Occidental)
- Shaikh Nawaf S. Al-Sabah (Deputy Chairman & Chief Executive Officer, Kuwait Petroleum Corporation)
- Scott D. Sheffield (CEO, Pioneer Natural Resources)
- Charif Souki (Executive Chairman, Tellurian Inc.)
- Moderated by Helima Croft (Managing Director and Head of Global Commodity Strategy and MENA Research, RBC Capital Markets)

9:27:04 - Closing Remarks | Melissa Lott (Director of Research and Senior Research Scholar, Center on Global Energy Policy)

localized.title: Columbia Global Energy Summit 2023
localized.description:
To kick off the celebration of our 10th anniversary, CGEP hosted a special 10th Anniversary Global Energy Summit — where we convened some of the brightest minds and important leaders to discuss turbulence in global energy markets, the growing gap between climate ambition and reality, and the tensions between today’s energy needs and tomorrow’s energy transition imperative.

0:37:59 - Welcome Remarks | Keren Yarhi-Milo (Dean, School of International and Public Affairs (SIPA), Adlai E. Stevenson Professor of International Relations, Columbia University)

0:45:00 - Opening Remarks | Jason Bordoff (Founding Director, Center on Global Energy Policy; Professor of Professional Practice, Columbia SIPA; Co-Founding Dean, Columbia Climate School)

0:53:50 - Panel | Power Play: The State of Energy Geopolitics Amidst Tensions and Transitions
- Ian Bremmer (President, Eurasia Group and GZERO Media)
- Meghan L. O'Sullivan (Director-designate, Belfer Center for Science and International Affairs and Jeane Kirkpatrick Professor of the Practice of International Affairs)
- Ana Palacio (Former Foreign Minister of Spain)
- Moderated by Daniel Yergin (Vice Chairman, IHS Markit)

1:37:55 - Building America's New Energy Future: A Conversation with Jennifer M. Granholm, Secretary, U.S. Department of Energy
- Moderated by Jason Bordoff

2:09:35 - Panel | Mobilizing Global Climate Action: Accelerating The Path to Net Zero by 2050
- Manish Bapna (CEO & President, NRDC)
- Representative John Curtis (US Congress)
- Fred Krupp (President, Environmental Defense Fund)
- Laurence Tubiana (CEO, European Climate Foundation)
- Moderated by Lisa Friedman (New York Times)

3:06:11 - Balancing Growth, Sustainability, and Access to Power Africa: A Conversation with H.E. Paul Kagame, President of the Republic of Rwanda
- Moderated by Jason Bordoff

3:47:05 - Panel | Building a Sustainable Future in Developing Countries
- Andrew Kamau (Managing Director of International Programs, Energy Opportunity Lab, Center on Global Energy Policy)
- Damilola Ogunbiyi (CEO of Sustainable Energy for All , Special Representative, UN Secretary-General for Sustainable Energy for All, and Co-Chair, UN-Energy)
- Hon. Okaasai Sidronius Opolot (Minister of State for Energy, Republic of Uganda)
- Moderated by Jonathan Elkind (Senior Research Scholar, Center on Global Energy Policy)

4:23:23 - Panel | Innovating to Net Zero: The Role of Technology in the Energy Transition
- Emmanuel Lagarrigue (Partner, KKR Infrastructure)
- Dr. Arun Majumdar (Chester Naramore Dean of the Stanford Doerr School of Sustainability, Jay Precourt Professor of Mechanical Engineering and of Energy Science & Engineering, and Senior Fellow of the Precourt Institute for Energy and of the Hoover Institution)
- Ann Mettler (Vice-President, Europe, Breakthrough Energy)
- Nobuo Tanaka (Chair, Steering Committee, Innovation for Cool Earth Forum, Executive Director Emeritus, the International Energy Agency (IEA) and former Distinguished Fellow, Center on Global Energy Policy, Columbia University)
- Moderated by Vijay Vaitheeswaran (Global Energy & Climate Innovation Editor, The Economist)

5:49:33 - Financing the Global Energy Transition: A Conversation with Larry Fink, Chairman and Chief Executive Officer, BlackRock, Inc.
- Moderated by Jason Bordoff

6:25:35 - Navigating the Disorderly Energy Transition: A Conversation with Fatih Birol, Executive Director, IEA
- Moderated by Jason Bordoff

6:58:24 - The Role of Today's Largest Companies in Tomorrow’s Energy Landscape: A Conversation with Patrick Pouyanné, Chairman of the Board and Chief Executive Officer, TotalEnergies
- Moderated by Jason Bordoff

7:39:13 - Panel | The Big Green Machine: Advancing the Energy Transition at Scale
- Mads Nipper (Group President and CEO, Ørsted)
- Christian Bruch (CEO, Siemens Energy)
- Moderated by Amy Harder (Executive Editor, Cipher News)

8:08:37 - Panel | Meeting the Energy and Climate Needs of Emerging Economies
- HE Leila Benali (Minister of Energy Transition and Sustainable Development, Government of Morocco)
- MA Jun (Founding Director, Institute of Public and Environmental Affairs)
- Sumant Sinha (Chairman & CEO, ReNew Power)
- Moderated by Akshat Rathi (Bloomberg)

8:45:16 - Panel | The Global State of Play: Energy Markets, Geopolitics, Policy, and What's Next
- Vicki Hollub (President and Chief Executive Officer, Occidental)
- Shaikh Nawaf S. Al-Sabah (Deputy Chairman & Chief Executive Officer, Kuwait Petroleum Corporation)
- Scott D. Sheffield (CEO, Pioneer Natural Resources)
- Charif Souki (Executive Chairman, Tellurian Inc.)
- Moderated by Helima Croft (Managing Director and Head of Global Commodity Strategy and MENA Research, RBC Capital Markets)

9:27:04 - Closing Remarks | Melissa Lott (Director of Research and Senior Research Scholar, Center on Global Energy Policy)

snippet.title: Warren Buffett:  "We like Occidental's position in the Permian Basin" | Warren Buffett talks on Oil
snippet.description:
Welcome to our channel! In this insightful video, we explore Warren Buffett's perspective on the oil industry, with a specific focus on Occidental Petroleum Corporation and its position in the Permian Basin. Join us as we dive into Buffett's thoughts and gain valuable insights into his investment rationale.

In this video, we examine Warren Buffett's views on the oil sector, highlighting his interest in Occidental Petroleum Corporation's strategic positioning in the highly productive Permian Basin. Buffett's investment decisions are often guided by a thorough analysis of a company's competitive advantage, long-term potential, and industry trends. By understanding his perspective, we can gain valuable insights into his investment strategies.

Throughout the video, we discuss the factors that make Occidental Petroleum Corporation an attractive investment opportunity. We explore the Permian Basin's vast oil reserves, its potential for long-term profitability, and Occidental's competitive advantage within this lucrative region. By examining Buffett's approach, we can gain a deeper understanding of how he identifies promising investments in the oil industry.

Moreover, we provide relevant data, statistics, and case studies to support our analysis. We also address potential risks and challenges that investors should be aware of when considering investments in the oil sector.

Whether you're a seasoned investor or simply curious about Warren Buffett's investment insights, this video offers valuable knowledge and perspectives. snippet.title: Nike Chief Human Resources Officer Monique Matheson on Supporting Employees and Social Good
snippet.description:
Dean Lloyd Minor welcomes NIKE Inc.’s Chief Human Resources Officer Monique Matheson for a conversation about how the pandemic changed the employee-employer relationship, how businesses can build high-functioning teams, and the power of speaking out for social justice from the platform of an international brand. They also discuss how sports principles can enhance employee synergy, the importance of supporting mental health and wellness, and why accountability is critical for making meaningful progress in workplace diversity, equity, and inclusion.

Subscribe: TheMinorConsult.com

About The Minor Consult Podcast 
The Minor Consult explores what it means to be a great leader in times of great uncertainty. Hosted by Stanford School of Medicine Dean Lloyd Minor, MD, the podcast convenes top minds from across fields to share their perspectives, impart lessons from their careers, and discuss the complex challenges leaders face today. Through their conversations, Dr. Minor unearths the qualities and skills that leaders need to succeed in turbulent times. 

Dr. Minor has served as Dean since 2012 and is also a professor of Otolaryngology–Head and Neck Surgery at the Stanford School of Medicine. Under his leadership, Stanford Medicine has emerged as a leader in the Precision Health revolution, which emphasizes preventive, personalized health care and leverages advances in biomedicine to treat and cure complex diseases. With more than 160 published articles and chapters, Dr. Minor is an expert in balance and inner ear disorders. In 2012, he was elected to the National Academy of Medicine. 

Disclaimer: Lloyd Minor has no financial interests in or formal relationships with the organizations of his guests.

.
.
.

Stanford Medicine advances human health through world-class biomedical research, education and patient care. Bringing together the resources of Stanford University School of Medicine, Stanford Health Care and Lucile Packard Children's Hospital, Stanford Medicine is committed to training future leaders in biomedicine and translating the latest discoveries into new ways to prevent, diagnose and treat disease.

The Stanford Medicine YouTube channel is a curated collection of contributions from our School of Medicine departments, divisions, students, and the community. Our diverse content includes coverage of events, presentations, lectures, and associated stories about the people of Stanford Medicine.

Stanford Medicine advances human health through world-class biomedical research, education and patient care. Bringing together the resources of Stanford University School of Medicine, Stanford Health Care and Lucile Packard Children's Hospital, Stanford Medicine is committed to training future leaders in biomedicine and translating the latest discoveries into new ways to prevent, diagnose and treat disease.

The Stanford Medicine YouTube channel is a curated collection of contributions from our School of Medicine departments, divisions, students, and the community. Our diverse content includes coverage of events, presentations, lectures, and associated stories about the people of Stanford Medicine.

snippet.title: Essex Property Trust Maintaining Capital Allocation Discipline
snippet.description:
Angela Kleiman, president and CEO of Essex Property Trust, Inc. (NYSE: ESS), participated in a video interview at Nareit’s REITweek: 2023 Investor Conference, held in New York June 6-8.

Kleiman discussed her priorities for the company in her new role as CEO, fundamentals in Essex’s core West Coast markets, including the supply outlook and whether Essex is seeing an impact on leasing levels from layoffs in the tech sector, how active Essex is likely to be on the acquisition, disposition, and development front for the remainder of 2023, and the outlook for the multifamily sector more broadly.
#REITsBolivia #REITs
